entries.text: SPX is 7,137.9, up 1.04%, while VIX still sits at 19.12 instead of flushing lower. That pairing still looks like controlled risk appetite rather than a full surrender in hedging demand.

Resettable Treasury paper still pays 3.628% and Total Marketable debt pays 3.365%, leaving a 26bp premium inside the same table. Short-duration funding is still richer than the overall book, so the curve is not behaving like pressure has disappeared.

TIPS are still at 0.999% while Treasury Bonds print 3.392%, so the nominal-real gap remains about 239bp in the same public table. That spread says inflation compensation is still embedded in the long end rather than washed out.

The VVIX gauge is 98.73 after a 101.75 high, which gives a more textured read than the headline VIX alone. The vol-of-vol measure is backing off, but it is doing so from an already elevated neighborhood, so the market still looks cautious rather than fully relaxed. That makes the observation useful as a state read instead of another generic risk-on slogan.
